User.title: User
User.description: Represents an authenticated user in the system with their assigned role and basic organizational details. This entity handles login identities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for authentication. (format: email)

role.description: The primary role assigned to the user, determining their access level.

departmentId.description: Reference to the Department this user is primarily associated with, if applicable. (Relationship: Department 1:N User)

designationId.description: Reference to the Designation (job title) of this user, if applicable. (Relationship: Designation 1:N User)

joiningDate.description: The date when the user joined the organization, if applicable. (format: date)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user record was created. (format: date-time)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user record was last updated. (format: date-time)

Employee.title: Employee
Employee.description: Contains detailed HR-specific information for individuals employed by the organization. A User with an 'Employee', 'HR', or 'Manager' role typically has an associated Employee record.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Employee entity.

userId.description: Reference to the associated User account. (Relationship: User 1:1 Employee)

employeeCode.description: A unique internal code or ID assigned to the employee by the organization.

firstName.description: The first name of the employee.

lastName.description: The last name of the employee.

dateOfBirth.description: The employee's date of birth. (format: date)

contactNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the employee.

address.description: The current residential address of the employee.

emergencyContactName.description: The name of an emergency contact person for the employee.

emergencyContactNumber.description: The phone number of the emergency contact person.

bankAccountNumber.description: The employee's bank account number for payroll deposits.

taxIdentifier.description: The employee's tax identification number (e.g., SSN, PAN).

managerId.description: Reference to another Employee who is this employee's direct manager. (Relationship: Employee N:1 Employee - self-referencing)

status.description: The current employment status of the employee (e.g., 'active', 'on_leave', 'terminated').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the employee record was created. (format: date-time)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the employee record was last updated. (format: date-time)

Department.title: Department
Department.description: Represents an organizational department within the company.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Department entity.

name.description: The official name of the department.

description.description: A brief description of the department's function.

headOfDepartmentId.description: Reference to the Employee who is the head of this department. (Relationship: Employee 1:N Department)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the department record was created. (format: date-time)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the department record was last updated. (format: date-time)

Designation.title: Designation
Designation.description: Represents a job title or position within the company.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Designation entity.

name.description: The official name of the job designation (e.g., 'Software Engineer', 'HR Manager').

description.description: A description of the responsibilities and requirements for this designation.

level.description: The seniority level of this designation (e.g., 'Entry', 'Mid', 'Senior', 'Lead', 'Executive').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the designation record was created. (format: date-time)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the designation record was last updated. (format: date-time)

Attendance.title: Attendance
Attendance.description: Records an employee's daily attendance, including check-in and check-out times.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Attendance entity.

employeeId.description: Reference to the Employee whose attendance is being recorded. (Relationship: Employee 1:N Attendance)

date.description: The date for which the attendance is recorded. (format: date)

checkInTime.description: The timestamp when the employee checked in. (format: date-time)

checkOutTime.description: The timestamp when the employee checked out. Optional if the employee is still checked in. (format: date-time)

status.description: The attendance status for the day (e.g., 'present', 'absent', 'leave', 'holiday', 'half_day').

notes.description: Any additional notes regarding the attendance record.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the attendance record was created. (format: date-time)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the attendance record was last updated. (format: date-time)

LeaveType.title: LeaveType
LeaveType.description: Defines different categories of leave available to employees.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LeaveType entity.

name.description: The name of the leave type (e.g., 'Sick Leave', 'Annual Leave', 'Maternity Leave').

description.description: A description of the leave type, including policies and eligibility.

accrualRate.description: The rate at which this leave type accrues (e.g., 1.5 days per month).

maxDaysPerYear.description: The maximum number of days allowed for this leave type per year.

requiresApproval.description: Indicates whether this leave type requires explicit approval from a manager or HR.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the leave type record was created. (format: date-time)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the leave type record was last updated. (format: date-time)

LeaveRequest.title: LeaveRequest
LeaveRequest.description: Represents an employee's formal request for a period of leave.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LeaveRequest entity.

employeeId.description: Reference to the Employee who submitted the leave request. (Relationship: Employee 1:N LeaveRequest)

leaveTypeId.description: Reference to the type of leave being requested. (Relationship: LeaveType 1:N LeaveRequest)

startDate.description: The start date of the requested leave period. (format: date)

endDate.description: The end date of the requested leave period. (format: date)

reason.description: The reason provided by the employee for requesting leave.

status.description: The current status of the leave request (e.g., 'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'cancelled').

appliedDate.description: Timestamp when the leave request was submitted by the employee. (format: date-time)

approvedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who approved or rejected the leave request. (Relationship: User 1:N LeaveRequest)

approvalDate.description: Timestamp when the leave request was approved or rejected. (format: date-time)

managerNotes.description: Notes added by the manager or HR during the approval process.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the leave request record was created. (format: date-time)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the leave request record was last updated. (format: date-time)

Payroll.title: Payroll
Payroll.description: Stores details of employee salary payments for a specific pay period.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payroll entity.

employeeId.description: Reference to the Employee for whom this payroll record is generated. (Relationship: Employee 1:N Payroll)

payPeriodStartDate.description: The start date of the pay period covered by this payroll. (format: date)

payPeriodEndDate.description: The end date of the pay period covered by this payroll. (format: date)

baseSalary.description: The employee's base salary for the defined pay period.

grossAmount.description: The total earnings before any deductions for the pay period.

deductionsTotal.description: The total amount deducted from the gross salary (e.g., taxes, provident fund).

bonusesTotal.description: The total amount of bonuses or additional payments for the pay period.

netAmount.description: The final amount paid to the employee after all calculations (gross - deductions + bonuses).

currency.description: The currency in which the payroll is processed (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

paymentDate.description: The date when the payroll payment was issued. (format: date)

status.description: The processing status of the payroll (e.g., 'generated', 'approved', 'paid', 'failed').

generatedByUserId.description: Reference to the User (Admin or HR) who generated this payroll record. (Relationship: User 1:N Payroll)

approvedByUserId.description: Reference to the User (Admin or HR) who approved this payroll record. (Relationship: User 1:N Payroll)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the payroll record was created. (format: date-time)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the payroll record was last updated. (format: date-time)

JobPosting.title: JobPosting
JobPosting.description: Details for a job vacancy advertised by the organization for recruitment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobPosting entity.

title.description: The title of the job vacancy (e.g., 'Senior Software Engineer').

description.description: A comprehensive description of the job role and its context within the company.

requirements.description: A list or summary of required skills, qualifications, and experience for the role.

responsibilities.description: A list or summary of the key duties and responsibilities associated with the role.

location.description: The geographical location of the job (e.g., 'Remote', 'New York, NY', 'On-site').

departmentId.description: Reference to the Department that has this job opening. (Relationship: Department 1:N JobPosting)

designationId.description: Reference to the Designation that this job posting is for. (Relationship: Designation 1:N JobPosting)

salaryRangeMin.description: The minimum expected annual salary for this role.

salaryRangeMax.description: The maximum expected annual salary for this role.

status.description: The current status of the job posting (e.g., 'open', 'closed', 'draft', 'on_hold').

postedByUserId.description: Reference to the User (HR) who created this job posting. (Relationship: User 1:N JobPosting)

postedDate.description: Timestamp when the job posting was published. (format: date-time)

closingDate.description: The deadline for submitting applications for this job. (format: date)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the job posting record was created. (format: date-time)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the job posting record was last updated. (format: date-time)

Candidate.title: Candidate
Candidate.description: Contains information about an applicant for a job posting, including their application details and resume content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Candidate entity.

jobPostingId.description: Reference to the JobPosting the candidate applied for. (Relationship: JobPosting 1:N Candidate)

firstName.description: The first name of the candidate.

lastName.description: The last name of the candidate.

email.description: The email address of the candidate. (format: email)

contactNumber.description: The contact phone number of the candidate.

resumeText.description: The full text content extracted from the candidate's resume, used for AI processing.

coverLetterText.description: The full text content of the candidate's cover letter.

status.description: The current application status of the candidate (e.g., 'applied', 'in_review', 'shortlisted', 'interviewing', 'offered', 'rejected', 'hired').

applicationDate.description: Timestamp when the candidate submitted their application. (format: date-time)

aiSummary.description: An AI-generated summary of the candidate's resume.

feedback.description: Internal feedback or notes from recruiters/hiring managers regarding the candidate.

lastUpdatedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who last updated this candidate's record. (Relationship: User 1:N Candidate)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the candidate record was created. (format: date-time)

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the candidate record was last updated. (format: date-time)

definition.description: Stores user authentication and profile information. The 'id' field matches the Firestore 'userId' (request.auth.uid). The 'role' field is for application logic; security rules rely on dedicated role collections for authorization. Includes denormalized 'departmentId' and 'designationId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Dedicated collection to grant 'Super Admin' role via document existence. Used for robust, atomic rule checks (Existence over Content) for Super Admin permissions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Dedicated collection to grant 'Admin' role via document existence. Used for robust, atomic rule checks (Existence over Content) for Admin permissions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Dedicated collection to grant 'HR' role via document existence. Used for robust, atomic rule checks (Existence over Content) for HR permissions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Dedicated collection to grant 'Manager' role via document existence. Used for robust, atomic rule checks (Existence over Content) for Manager permissions.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Dedicated collection to grant 'Employee' role via document existence. Used for robust, atomic rule checks (Existence over Content) for Employee permissions. This might be a default role.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores detailed HR-specific information for employees. Includes 'userId' to link to the User account and 'managerId' for hierarchical reporting. 'departmentId' and 'designationId' are references.

params.name: employeeId
params.description: The unique ID of the employee.

definition.description: Represents an organizational department. Includes 'headOfDepartmentId' (reference to Employee) and denormalized 'headOfDepartmentUserId' for authorization independence.

params.name: departmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the department.

definition.description: Represents a job title or position within the company. Globally accessible for HR/Admin, viewable by others.

params.name: designationId
params.description: The unique ID of the designation.

definition.description: Defines different categories of leave. Globally accessible for HR/Admin, viewable by employees to apply for leave.

params.name: leaveTypeId
params.description: The unique ID of the leave type.

definition.description: Records an employee's daily attendance. Includes denormalized 'employeeUserId', 'employeeManagerId', and 'employeeDepartmentId' for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: attendanceId
params.description: The unique ID of the attendance record.

definition.description: Represents an employee's formal leave request. Includes denormalized 'employeeUserId', 'employeeManagerId', 'employeeDepartmentId', and 'approvedByUserId' for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: leaveRequestId
params.description: The unique ID of the leave request.

definition.description: Stores details of employee salary payments. Includes denormalized 'employeeUserId', 'employeeDepartmentId', 'generatedByUserId', and 'approvedByUserId' for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: payrollId
params.description: The unique ID of the payroll record.

definition.description: Details for a job vacancy. Includes 'postedByUserId' for ownership and 'status' (e.g., 'open', 'draft') to facilitate QAPs for public viewing and HR management. 'departmentId' and 'designationId' are references.

params.name: jobPostingId
params.description: The unique ID of the job posting.

definition.description: Contains applicant information. Denormalizes 'jobPostingDepartmentId' and 'jobPostingPostedByUserId' from the associated JobPosting for authorization independence and QAPs (e.g., HR/Managers viewing candidates for specific departments or postings).

params.name: candidateId
params.description: The unique ID of the candidate.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for the WorkNest HR system is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Queryable Authorization Patterns (QAPs) to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved primarily through pervasive denormalization. Rather than relying on `get()` operations in security rules (which are complex, prone to breaking atomic operations, and difficult to debug), critical authorization context is copied directly into the documents. For instance, `Attendance`, `LeaveRequest`, and `Payroll` documents, which relate to an `Employee`, will not only store `employeeId` but also denormalize `employeeUserId` (the `userId` linked to that employee's profile) and `employeeManagerId` (the `employeeId` of their direct manager). Similarly, `Candidate` documents will denormalize `jobPostingDepartmentId` and `jobPostingPostedByUserId` from the associated `JobPosting`. Roles themselves are handled using an 'Existence over Content' pattern: dedicated top-level collections like `/user_roles_admin/{userId}` exist. The mere presence of a document at such a path grants the user that role, allowing for direct and atomic rule checks like `if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/user_roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` without needing to read the user's full profile.

**Queryable Authorization Patterns (QAPs):** This structure ensures that `list` operations remain secure and efficient. 
*   **Structural Segregation** is used to group documents with similar security needs. For example, `JobPosting` documents for public viewing will have a `status: 'open'`, allowing a single collection with query filters for public access, while HR/Admins can view all statuses. 
*   For employee-specific data (e.g., `Attendance`, `LeaveRequest`, `Payroll`), instead of deep nesting, these are placed in top-level collections. Each document includes denormalized `employeeUserId` and `employeeManagerId`. This allows an employee to securely `list` their own records (`where('employeeUserId', '==', request.auth.uid)`) and managers to `list` their team's records (`where('employeeManagerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`) without performing cross-document `get()` calls in the security rules. HR/Admins can list all records by checking their role via the dedicated role collections.

**Summary of Path Structure & Denormalization:**
*   **`/users/{userId}`**: Stores core user profile. The `role` field here is for application logic, while actual rule authorization uses dedicated role collections.
*   **Role Collections (`/user_roles_superAdmin/{userId}`, etc.)**: Implement DBAC via 'Existence over Content' for robust, atomic role checks in security rules.
*   **`/employees/{employeeId}`**: Contains core HR data. `userId` (linking to `/users/{userId}`) and `managerId` are present. `departmentId` and `designationId` are references.
*   **`/departments/{departmentId}`**: Organizational units. `headOfDepartmentUserId` is denormalized from the `Employee` record of the head, enabling direct authorization checks for department heads.
*   **`/designations/{designationId}` & `/leaveTypes/{leaveTypeId}`**: Global, reference data primarily managed by HR/Admins.
*   **`/jobPostings/{jobPostingId}`**: Job vacancy details. `postedByUserId` is denormalized for creator-based access control. The `status` field (`open`, `draft`) enables QAPs for public listing.
*   **`/candidates/{candidateId}`**: Applicant information. Denormalizes `jobPostingId`, `jobPostingDepartmentId`, and `jobPostingPostedByUserId` to allow HR/Manager to query and manage candidates relevant to specific job postings or departments without additional `get()` calls.
*   **`/attendances/{attendanceId}`**, **`/leaveRequests/{leaveRequestId}`**, **`/payrolls/{payrollId}`**: These collections each include denormalized `employeeUserId`, `employeeManagerId`, and `employeeDepartmentId`. This design allows for efficient, secure `list` queries by employees (for their own records), by managers (for their team's records), and by HR/Admins (for all or department-specific records), all while maintaining Authorization Independence by avoiding `get()` calls.

This architecture ensures that security rules are straightforward, permissions are explicit, and the system can scale effectively while providing strong data integrity and debuggability.
